mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return hit rate in PAYEMS-up months is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating household income expectations and expanding equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal triggers but S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return during trigger periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm a broad demand-supply expansion that exceeds market expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO-up and RSAFS-up months fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term does not significantly improve predictive R-squared (incremental p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime, month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) compresses real interest rates and reflates nominal corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS < 0.25% regimes, accelerating CPIAUCSL months produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the strategy Sharpe ratio underperforms the buy-and-hold baseline over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) validates consumer spending momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth months fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean 5-day return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeding 25 basis points signals a shift to restrictive monetary policy, raising equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS increase > 0.25pp months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return during these months is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirm a supply-side recovery with labor market absorption, jointly generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises MoM and UNRATE falls MoM
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO-up and UNRATE-down months fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term does not significantly improve predictive R-squared (incremental p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1.0 percent signals capacity underutilization and corporate margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO decline > 1.0% months are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return during these months is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
